visualization.name: Sequence Viewer
visualization.description: Visualize DNA, RNA, and protein sequences using SeqViz

input.label: Viewer Mode
input.help: Select how the sequence is displayed.
input.name: viewer

input.label: Show Annotations
input.help: Toggle annotation lines in the visualization.
input.name: show_annotation_lines

visualization.help:
# What is SeqViz Viewer?
The SeqViz Viewer provides an **interactive sequence visualization** powered by the [SeqViz](https://github.com/Lattice-Automation/seqviz) library. It displays DNA, RNA, and protein sequences in linear or circular view with full annotation support.
After selecting a dataset, the viewer renders the sequence with features such as:
- **Multiple Views**: Switch between linear, circular, or both layouts - **Annotations**: Display feature annotations, labels, and highlights - **Interactive Controls**: Zoom, pan, and navigate sequence features - **Multiple Formats**: Supports FASTA, GenBank, and other common sequence formats
This tool is ideal for visualizing plasmids, genes, genomes, and protein sequences.
Learn more about SeqViz at: [https://github.com/Lattice-Automation/seqviz](https://github.com/Lattice-Automation/seqviz)
